content.claim: In July 2023, the Hungarian government enacted a unified legal status law for teachers across all provider types (state, church, private, foundation schools) without adequate union consultation, restricting strike rights and granting employers unilateral authority to reassign educators to different schools to address teacher shortages.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Political feasibility lens
limitations: The specific provisions and their implementation pace are not itemized in these notes
Union characterization of the law as 'retaliation' is a contested framing, though the factual occurrence and content of unilateral passage is confirmed
The long-term impact on SEN specialist retention or recruitment is not yet measured
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
